title: Suspicious-Looking but Legitimate Rush Invoice
benchmark_purpose: Test false-positive resistance. This invoice has multiple surface-level fraud indicators — urgent language, unusual amount, off-cadence timing, expedited payment request — but every detail is verifiable as clean from the payload. The test is whether the system can distinguish genuine urgency from manufactured urgency.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ESCALATE — urgency language + unusual amount + off-cadence timing triggers multiple escalation criteria simultaneously. A system that weights surface signals without cross-referencing will false-positive.

email_thread.subject: URGENT — Emergency Office Setup for Westfield Expansion
email_thread.body:
Dana,

We're accelerating the Westfield office expansion. We need a full office setup for 40 workstations by March 20. This is on an emergency PO — PO-TC-9250-RUSH.

Can you provide a quote today? We need desks, chairs, monitors, and supplies. Budget approved up to $45K.

Tom Nguyen
Facilities Director

email_thread.subject: RE: URGENT — Emergency Office Setup for Westfield Expansion
email_thread.body:
Hi Tom,

We can absolutely help. Here's what we can deliver by March 20:

- 40x adjustable desks: $16,000
- 40x ergonomic chairs: $12,000
- 40x monitor arms: $3,200
- Bulk supplies package: $10,000

Total: $41,200.00

Given the rush timeline, we'd need Net-15 terms instead of our usual Net-30. We need to pre-order the desks from our supplier this week.

I'll send the formal invoice once you confirm.

Best,
Dana Kessler
Meridian Office Supplies LLC
(555) 312-4400

email_thread.subject: RE: URGENT — Emergency Office Setup for Westfield Expansion
email_thread.body:
Dana — confirmed. Please proceed and send the invoice.

Tom

email_thread.subject: Invoice MOS-2026-0365-RUSH — Emergency Westfield Setup
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Attached is invoice MOS-2026-0365-RUSH for the emergency Westfield office setup per PO-TC-9250-RUSH, as approved by Tom Nguyen.

Amount: $41,200.00. Terms: Net-15 (per agreement due to rush timeline).

Please note this is in addition to our regular monthly supplies invoice.

Best,
Dana Kessler
Accounts Receivable
Meridian Office Supplies LLC
(555) 312-4400

attachments.content_summary: Invoice from Meridian Office Supplies LLC. MOS-2026-0365-RUSH. PO: PO-TC-9250-RUSH. 40x adjustable desks ($16,000), 40x ergonomic chairs ($12,000), 40x monitor arms ($3,200), bulk supplies ($10,000). Total: $41,200.00. Net-15. Bank: First National Bank, routing 071000013, account ****7834.

anomalies: Amount $41,200 is 189% above the 6-month average of ~$14,100
Payment terms Net-15 instead of usual Net-30

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Legitimate rush order. The email thread shows the internal request originated from TargetCorp's Facilities Director (Tom Nguyen), not from the vendor. The vendor responded to an internal request. The bank account matches. The domain matches. Email auth passes. The originating IP is the same as the vendor's historical IP. The amount is high because it's a bulk emergency order, not a recurring supply invoice. The payment terms changed because the vendor explained the rush timeline requires advance supplier ordering. The approval chain is complete — all three required approvers signed off. The PO (emergency PO-TC-9250-RUSH) is referenced. Every suspicious surface signal has a legitimate explanation documented in the thread.

evidence_signals.signal: Full email thread documents the request/response flow
Amount explained by bulk emergency order, not recurring supply
Term change explained in thread (rush supplier ordering)

scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Very high — this scenario triggers multiple escalation criteria simultaneously: amount >120% of average, urgency language, off-cadence timing, changed payment terms. A system that treats these as independent risk factors and sums them will over-trigger.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Strong differentiator. A solo one-pass system applying escalation criteria mechanically will ESCALATE. A multi-pass system that reads the full thread and understands that the urgency originated internally (not from the vendor) should ALLOW. A system with compounding postmortems should note: 'the amount is unusual BUT the request originated internally AND the bank hasn't changed AND the approvals are complete — the suspicious signals are explained by context.'

notes_for_humans: This is the hardest false-positive trap. It hits multiple escalation criteria simultaneously — by design. The key insight is that every suspicious signal has a legitimate contextual explanation documented in the thread. The urgency came from inside the organization. The system must read the thread to understand causation, not just pattern-match on surface signals.
